title: Right-censored time-to-next-pit model converted back to P(next lap)
summary: Survival formulations are a natural fit for 'when is the next stop?' and official XGBoost AFT support handles censoring directly.
rationale: Survival formulations are a natural fit for 'when is the next stop?' and official XGBoost AFT support handles censoring directly. Adjacent literature on discrete-time survival finds hazard parameterizations strong for event-time prediction, which matches the pit-window nature of this problem better than a single binary snapshot.
implementation_hint: For each row, compute laps until the driver’s next pit stop within the race. Use uncensored intervals `[t,t]` when a future stop exists and right-censored `[t,+inf)` when it does not. Train an XGBoost `survival:aft` expert or a discrete-hazard multi-bin model over 1/2/3/5 laps, then convert to `P(T=1)` or `h(1)` for submission and stacking.
expected_effect: Can sharpen pit-window timing and improve consistency across adjacent laps, especially near stint ends.
risk: Label construction is easy to get subtly wrong around final stints and already-pit rows; local CV may be noisy if conversion from survival output to next-lap probability is crude.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
